---
title: "The Discretionary Power of Parole" 
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    embed-resources: TRUE
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
    fig_caption: yes
    toc: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev = "ragg_png"
)

source("R/config.R")

# Load prepared data
load(file = paste0(config$sp_data_path, "/data/analysis/app/filtered_parole_elig_table_analysis_year.rds"))
load(file = paste0(config$sp_data_path, "/data/analysis/app/map_percent.rds"))

num_people_current <- sum(filtered_parole_elig_table_analysis_year$current_count, na.rm = TRUE)

# <div id="wide-section">
# </div>
# <style>
# @media (max-width: 768px) {
#   #wide-section {
#     display: none; /* Hide wide-section on mobile */
#   }
# }
# </style>
```
<br>
<div id="body-section">
In the United States, parole boards hold significant power over the lives of incarcerated people eligible for parole. While 16 states have abolished parole release for crimes committed after a certain date, 34 states still rely on parole boards' discretion to decide when people can be supervised in the community.

```{r}
#![Combined Map](combined_map_high_res.png)
# People in Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Date
```

Nationally, over 88,000 people are in prison past their parole eligibility date, highlighting the critical role that parole boards play in determining the timing of release. 

</div>
<div id="narrow-section">
```{r}
div(
  id = "grid-container",
  style = "display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);",
  div(id = "highlight-text", "XX%"),
  div(id = "highlight-text", formattable::comma(num_people_current, digits = 0)),
  div(id = "bold-text", HTML("Proportion of People in Prison<br>Past Their Parole Eligibility Date")),
  div(id = "bold-text", HTML("People in Prison<br>Past Their Parole Eligibility Date"))
)
```
</div>
<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
<div id="map-section">
```{r}
map_percent %>% 
  hc_size(height = 730) %>% 
    hc_exporting(enabled = TRUE,
               buttons = list(contextButton = list(align = 'right',
                                                   verticalAlign = 'top',
                                                   y = 0,
                                                   x = -5))) 

```
</div>

<div id="body-section">
<div class="divider"></div>
# The Power of Parole Boards

<div id="grid-container" style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 0px;">
  <p><br>
    Despite the critical role they play, parole boards are often composed of a small number of individuals. In many states, just 5-10 parole board members are responsible for decisions affecting thousands of people awaiting parole.<br><br>This concentration of power highlights the need for transparency and consistency in the parole decision-making process.<br><br>Parole board members must balance a range of factors, including public safety, the rehabilitation progress of incarcerated people, and the perspectives of victims and their families. These boards often work with limited resources and under significant public and political pressure.
  </p><br>
  ![](square_dot_parole_graphic.png)
</div>



<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
# Disparities in Parole Decisions

Research indicates significant racial, ethnic, and gender disparities in parole decisions. Black and Hispanic individuals are less likely to be granted parole compared to their white counterparts. Gender disparities also exist, with women facing different challenges and rates of releases to parole compared to men.  

 


<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
# Impact on Recidivism

Studies indicate that timely parole can reduce recidivism rates. People who are granted parole and receive proper support are less likely to re-offend.




<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
# Economic Impact of Parole

Effective parole systems can reduce incarceration costs and improve economic outcomes for individuals and communities.  
<br>

If incarceration costs and marginal costs are available, show them here. Graph with potential cost savings?

<br>


</div>
<br>
